beginner.question: What is an Field-Programmable Gate Array (FPGA)?
beginner.answer: A reconfigurable chip you program as custom hardware.

beginner.question: FPGA vs Microcontroller Unit (MCU)?
beginner.answer: FPGA is parallel hardware; MCU is sequential software.

intermediate.question: What is a bitstream?
intermediate.answer: The configuration loaded to set up the fabric.

advanced.question: Timing closure?
advanced.answer: Meeting setup/hold across the design at target clock.

expert.question: When choose FPGA?
expert.answer: When parallelism, latency, or I/O speed beat an MCU/Application-Specific Integrated Circuit (ASIC) tradeoff.
